name: Field
description: A form field wrapper that connects a label, description, input control, and validation error message with proper accessibility attributes. Built on Base UI Field with StyleX styling.

Root.description: Groups label, description, control, and error. Renders a flex-column container with space1 (4px) gap between parts (label → control, control → error, etc.).

invalid.description: Whether the field is in an invalid state. When true, Error children are shown and Control receives invalid styling.

disabled.description: Whether the entire field is disabled. Propagates to Control.

name.description: The name of the field, used when submitting a form.

dataAttributes.data-valid: Present when the field is valid.
dataAttributes.data-invalid: Present when the field is invalid.
dataAttributes.data-dirty: Present when the field value has been modified.
dataAttributes.data-touched: Present when the field has been focused and blurred.

Label.description: The label for the field. Automatically linked to Control via htmlFor.

dataAttributes.data-valid: Present when the field is valid.
dataAttributes.data-invalid: Present when the field is invalid.
dataAttributes.data-dirty: Present when the field value has been modified.
dataAttributes.data-touched: Present when the field has been focused and blurred.

Description.description: Hint text displayed below the label. Linked to Control via aria-describedby.

dataAttributes.data-valid: Present when the field is valid.
dataAttributes.data-invalid: Present when the field is invalid.
dataAttributes.data-dirty: Present when the field value has been modified.
dataAttributes.data-touched: Present when the field has been focused and blurred.

Error.description: Validation error message, shown when the field is invalid.

match.description: Match a specific ValidityState key (e.g., 'valueMissing', 'typeMismatch') or provide a custom function. Only shows the error when the matched condition is true.

forceShow.description: Whether to show the error message regardless of the field's validity state.

dataAttributes.data-valid: Present when the field is valid.
dataAttributes.data-invalid: Present when the field is invalid.
dataAttributes.data-dirty: Present when the field value has been modified.
dataAttributes.data-touched: Present when the field has been focused and blurred.

Control.description: The input control. Renders an <input> by default. Supports the render prop to swap the underlying element.

size.description: Size of the control affecting height, padding, and font size. Matches Button sizes (sm=32px, md=36px, lg=40px).

render.description: Swap the rendered element. Useful for rendering a <textarea> or custom input.

dataAttributes.data-valid: Present when the field is valid.
dataAttributes.data-invalid: Present when the field is invalid.
dataAttributes.data-dirty: Present when the field value has been modified.
dataAttributes.data-touched: Present when the field has been focused and blurred.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the control is disabled.

Validity.description: A render prop component that provides FieldValidityData (state, errors, value). Does not render any DOM element.

children.description: Render function receiving the current validity state, errors, and value.

intents.reasoning: Field is the standard wrapper for any form input that needs a label, description, and validation. It connects all parts with proper accessibility attributes.
intents.composition:
<Field.Root>
  <Field.Label>Email</Field.Label>
  <Field.Control type="email" placeholder="you@example.com" />
  <Field.Error>Please enter a valid email address.</Field.Error>
</Field.Root>

intents.reasoning: Field automatically handles native constraint validation and displays error messages matched to specific validity states.
intents.composition:
<Field.Root>
  <Field.Label>Username</Field.Label>
  <Field.Control required minLength={3} />
  <Field.Error match="valueMissing">Username is required.</Field.Error>
  <Field.Error match="tooShort">Username must be at least 3 characters.</Field.Error>
</Field.Root>

intents.reasoning: Field automatically links the label and description to the input via htmlFor and aria-describedby, ensuring full accessibility.

avoidWhen.scenario: Standalone label without an input control
avoidWhen.reasoning: Field is designed to connect a label to a control. For standalone labels or headings, use a regular <label> or heading element.
avoidWhen.alternative: Use a plain <label> element or heading.

avoidWhen.scenario: Complex multi-input groups (e.g., date with day/month/year fields)
avoidWhen.reasoning: Field wraps a single control. For groups of related inputs, use Fieldset which provides group-level labeling and validation.
avoidWhen.alternative: Use Fieldset to group multiple related inputs.

avoidWhen.reasoning: Field includes form semantics and validation wiring that are unnecessary for read-only data display.
avoidWhen.alternative: Use a description list (<dl>) or custom layout component.

examples.name: basic
examples.description: Basic text field with a label

examples.name: with-validation
examples.description: Field with native constraint validation and error messages
examples.code:
<Field.Root>
  <Field.Label>Email</Field.Label>
  <Field.Control type="email" required placeholder="you@example.com" />
  <Field.Error match="valueMissing">Email is required.</Field.Error>
  <Field.Error match="typeMismatch">Please enter a valid email address.</Field.Error>
</Field.Root>

examples.name: with-description
examples.description: Field with a description hint below the label
examples.code:
<Field.Root>
  <Field.Label>Password</Field.Label>
  <Field.Description>Must be at least 8 characters long.</Field.Description>
  <Field.Control type="password" required minLength={8} />
  <Field.Error match="valueMissing">Password is required.</Field.Error>
  <Field.Error match="tooShort">Password must be at least 8 characters.</Field.Error>
</Field.Root>
